message:
fix(target-home): expand passwd-based home resolution into every ACFS subsystem

Follow-on from `fix(target-home): ignore stale TARGET_HOME and prefer
on-disk resolution` — that commit hardened the target-user/target-home
resolution in the subsystems that were actively failing in the field;
this commit extends the same pattern into every remaining subsystem
the doctor / nightly_update / preflight / services-setup paths touch,
and upgrades the "is `ACFS_BIN_DIR` actually owned by the target
user?" check so an override that resolves to a different user's home
is rejected instead of silently writing cross-user binaries.

README + install.sh behavioural contract
----------------------------------------
- `README.md`: the `TARGET_HOME` default in the env-vars table is no
  longer "`/home/$TARGET_USER`". It now reads "Resolved from
  `TARGET_USER` (or explicit override)" — matching what the installer
  has actually been doing since the passwd-based resolver landed.
- `install.sh`: the comment above the `TARGET_HOME="${TARGET_HOME:-}"`
  export used to say "fall back to `/home/$TARGET_USER` as a last
  resort". That fallback was removed earlier — the comment is now
  "`init_target_paths` derives it from the real passwd entry when
  possible and otherwise fails closed". The contract is no longer
  "always produce a home" but "refuse to produce a bogus one", which
  matches the doctor / smoke_test invariants the rest of this patch
  tightens.

Per-subsystem resolver helpers
------------------------------
Every subsystem that had its own `_<prefix>_target_home` resolver
gets the matching `_<prefix>_read_user_for_home` helper — the
inverse lookup that returns the user owning a given absolute home
path, checked in this order:
1. `getent passwd` (iterated, match by home column).
2. Direct read from `/etc/passwd` (same iteration shape).
3. Fall back to `id -un` / `whoami` only when the requested home
   matches `_<prefix>_CURRENT_HOME`.
4. Hard-coded `root` when and only when the requested home is
   exactly `/root`.
5. State-file `target_user` if the home matches what that user's
   passwd entry resolves to.

Matching username validation everywhere uses the POSIX shape
`^[a-z_][a-z0-9._-]*$` so a passwd row with a malformed username
column can't sneak through.

Library files touched with this helper + the paired
`_<prefix>_existing_abs_nonroot_dir` / `_<prefix>_existing_abs_home`
gate from the earlier commit:

  scripts/lib/agents.sh
  scripts/lib/autofix.sh
  scripts/lib/cheatsheet.sh
  scripts/lib/cli_tools.sh
  scripts/lib/dashboard.sh
  scripts/lib/export-config.sh
  scripts/lib/github_api.sh
  scripts/lib/info.sh
  scripts/lib/install_helpers.sh
  scripts/lib/nightly_update.sh
  scripts/lib/os_detect.sh
  scripts/lib/smoke_test.sh
  scripts/lib/status.sh
  scripts/lib/support.sh
  scripts/lib/ubuntu_upgrade.sh
  scripts/lib/update.sh
  scripts/lib/user.sh

`stack.sh` — `ACFS_BIN_DIR` cross-user guard
--------------------------------------------
`_stack_target_bin_dir` previously accepted any absolute `ACFS_BIN_DIR`
override and printed it. Now:
- If the override is under the resolved target home (or exactly
  equals it), it's accepted as before.
- Otherwise, iterate `getent passwd` and reject the override if it
  resolves to any *other* user's home. The function then falls
  through to the default `$target_home/.local/bin`.
- An override that resolves to no user's home (e.g. `/opt/tools`)
  is still accepted — administrators can explicitly install into
  system dirs.

`_stack_target_home` now also consults `HOME` when `id -un` matches
the requested target user, so commands run by the target user's own
shell don't pay the extra passwd roundtrip when the answer is
already in the environment.

`doctor.sh` / `doctor_fix.sh` — bin-dir validation + remediation
----------------------------------------------------------------
- `doctor.sh` picks up `_acfs_doctor_existing_abs_nonroot_dir` and
  `_acfs_doctor_validate_bin_dir_for_home`. `ensure_path` now runs
  the override through the validator before expanding the candidate
  dirs list, so a path that fails validation is replaced with
  `$primary_home/.local/bin` instead of leaking into the final PATH
  fixture. +97 net lines.
- `doctor_fix.sh` gets the paired remediation flow: it can now
  diagnose a mismatched `ACFS_BIN_DIR` override and offer to rewrite
  it. +154 net lines.

Installers, preflight, services-setup
-------------------------------------
- `scripts/acfs-global` / `scripts/acfs-update`: +12 lines each for
  the helper-registration boilerplate so the direct-exec resolvers
  see the same validator set.
- `packages/onboard/onboard.sh`: +164 lines — onboarding now
  enforces the same "existing absolute non-root dir" gate on the
  ACFS home candidate list, and derives the target user from the
  resolved home via the new inverse lookup.
- `scripts/preflight.sh`: +97 lines — the disk-space partition
  probe now computes the target partition from the resolved home
  instead of the raw `TARGET_HOME` env var. Prevents the
  quota/space check from scanning the wrong mount when `TARGET_HOME`
  is stale.
- `scripts/services-setup.sh`: +102 lines — systemd unit templating
  picks up the resolved home via the new lookups.
- `scripts/tests/ubuntu_upgrade_test.sh`: +122 lines — adds the
  same helper surface under test harness so ubuntu-upgrade simulation
  exercises every branch of the new resolver.

Tests
-----
- `tests/unit/lib/test_update.bats` +530 lines and
  `tests/unit/test_changelog_export_status.sh` +873 lines — expanded
  regression coverage for the stale-TARGET_HOME, wrong-user-bin-dir,
  and "existing-dir-required" branches.
- `tests/unit/lib/test_install_helpers.bats`,
  `tests/unit/lib/test_os_detect.bats`,
  `tests/unit/lib/test_user.bats` — add the matching helper
  regression cases for the respective lib modules.
- `tests/unit/test_autofix.sh`, `tests/unit/test_doctor_fix.sh` —
  new end-to-end harnesses for the autofix / doctor_fix flows that
  now depend on the validated bin-dir pipeline.
- `tests/vm/test_install_artifacts.sh` — the VM-level install gate
  picks up the same validator-aware expectations so it fails fast
  when the installer regresses back to the pre-fix behaviour.

Net effect: every subsystem that cares about target-user identity
now routes through the same "existing absolute home" gate and the
same passwd-backed inverse lookup. A stale or deleted `TARGET_HOME`,
or an `ACFS_BIN_DIR` override that points at another user's home,
fails closed instead of silently picking up cross-user state.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
